w:r.w:t: She usually ___ to school at 8 a.m.
I was tired because I ___ all night.
She ___ for two hours. She needs a rest.
My father always ___ tea in the morning.
After she ___ home, she called me.
While I ___ , my brother was sleeping.
When we got to the station, the train ___ .

Emma had been preparing for her driving test for months. She had failed it once before, so this time she was determined to succeed. On the morning of the test, she felt confident because she had practiced every day that week. However, while she was driving during the exam, she made a small mistake at a roundabout. She thought she had failed again. Surprisingly, the examiner smiled at the end and told her she had passed. Emma realized that being calm had helped her more than being perfect.
Why was this test important for Emma?
A) It was her first lesson
D) She needed it for school
How did she feel before the test?
B) She argued with the examiner
C) She made a small mistake

Over the past few years, online learning has become more popular. Many students enjoy studying from home because it saves time and money. However, some students have found it difficult to stay focused. Without teachers physically present, they sometimes check their phones or leave tasks unfinished. Experts say that successful online learners usually create a daily schedule and avoid distractions. Those who treat online classes seriously often perform just as well as students in traditional classrooms.
Why do many students like online learning?
A) It is easier than school
B) It saves time and money
What problem do some students face?
C) They struggle to stay focused
D) They have too much homework
According to experts, what should students do?
What is the main idea of the text?
B) Online learning is always better
C) Success in online learning depends on discipline

When Daniel started his first job, he expected it to be exciting. Instead, the first few weeks were stressful. He had to learn new systems, remember people’s names, and manage tight deadlines. At one point, he even considered quitting. However, after a month, things began to change. He became more confident and started finishing tasks faster. Looking back, Daniel realized that the hardest part of any new experience is usually the beginning.
How did Daniel feel at the beginning?
Why did he think about quitting?
D) He moved to another city
B) New beginnings are often the hardest
